access.anon: Can see the subscription entry but cannot directly create a subscription.
access.user: Can only manage their own section subscription relationships.
access.admin: Does not manage personal subscriptions on behalf of users through ordinary pages.
access.agent: After OAuth authorization, can manage the current user's own subscription relationships; REST and MCP both support the same semantic operations.

rules.subscription-language: User-facing copy uses 'section subscription', 'subscribe', and 'unsubscribe' consistently; it must not use 'follow' or describe the action as course enrollment.
rules.user-scoped: Subscription relationships belong only to the current user and determine the display scope for home page, calendar, homework, and exam.
rules.bounded-batch-input: Calendar subscription query and mutation requests accept at most 500 section IDs and at most 500 lookup codes per request, in addition to the per-user batch request budget.

tools.name: workspace_subscription_add
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, action: String, sectionJwId: Int, subscription }
notes: Missing section jwId returns { success: false, action: "not_found", subscription: null }.
Default and the deprecated summary alias return a compact subscription update result instead of the full subscribed section list.
Request full mode only when the caller explicitly needs the full CalendarSubscription with sections[].

tools.name: workspace_subscription_remove
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, action: String, sectionJwId: Int, subscription }
notes: Missing section jwId returns { success: false, action: "not_found", subscription: null }.
Default and the deprecated summary alias return a compact subscription update result instead of the full subscribed section list.
Request full mode only when the caller explicitly needs the full CalendarSubscription with sections[].

mutations.name: subscriptionsImport
notes: Accepts at most 500 unique public section/course codes; internal section IDs are intentionally not exposed.
ADD and REMOVE require at least one code. SET requires semesterId and may use an empty code list to clear only that semester.
Unknown codes remain successful unmatchedCodes results, matching REST preview-and-apply semantics.

routes.returns: { semester, matchedCodes: String[], unmatchedCodes: String[], matchedSectionIds: Int[], unmatchedSectionIds: Int[], suggestions, sections: Section[], total: Int }
notes: Preferred batch query endpoint for calendar subscription picker flows.
Accepts sectionIds plus mixed section/course codes through codes[].
Code matching is semester-scoped; omitted semesterId uses the current semester.

routes.returns: { action, semester, matchedCodes, unmatchedCodes, matchedSectionIds, unmatchedSectionIds, sections, addedCount, removedCount, unchangedCount, subscription }
notes: Preferred mutation endpoint for batch subscribe, unsubscribe, and replace/update flows.
Accepts action plus sectionIds and/or mixed section/course codes.
action=set requires semesterId, replaces only that semester (an empty target clears it), and preserves every other semester.
action=set preserves a retired Section only when it is already subscribed and its ID is explicitly included; omitted or newly requested retired Sections remain unsubscribed.

routes.returns: { semester: Semester, matchedCodes: String[], unmatchedCodes: String[], suggestions: Record<String, String[]>, sections: Section[], total: Int }

notes: Compatibility replace endpoint for ID-only callers.
subscription.sections[] is the user's new set of subscribed sections.
A retired Section is preserved only when it is already subscribed and its ID is explicitly included; retired Sections cannot be newly subscribed.

routes.returns: { subscription: CalendarSubscription, addedCount: Int, alreadySubscribedCount: Int }
notes: Compatibility endpoint for ID-only callers; appends selected section IDs after preview without replacing the caller's whole subscription set.
Used by dashboard and welcome bulk-import confirmation after users deselect unwanted matched sections.
Unknown positive section IDs are ignored; malformed or non-positive IDs return 400.
Already-subscribed selected sections are counted but not duplicated.
Compatibility remove endpoint for ID-only callers.
Remove selected section IDs without replacing the caller's whole subscription set.
Used by dashboard opt-out controls so concurrent additions are not overwritten by stale client-side read-merge-replace flows.
Unknown positive section IDs are ignored; malformed or non-positive IDs return 400.

routes.returns: { success: Boolean, semester, matchedCodes, unmatchedCodes, ambiguousCodes, sections, addedCount, addedSections, alreadySubscribedCount, alreadySubscribedSections, subscription }
notes: Compatibility convenience endpoint that combines code matching with an append-only subscription mutation.
Codes may be section codes or course codes in the selected/current semester.
Does not replace the caller's whole subscription set, so concurrent additions are not overwritten by stale client-side read-merge-replace flows.

tools.name: catalog_section_match_preview
tools.returns: { semester: Semester, matchedCodes: String[], unmatchedCodes: String[], suggestions: Record<String, String[]>, sections: Section[], total: Int }
notes: Returns suggestions for unmatched inputs when close section codes in the selected semester share a strong code-prefix or edit-distance match; unrelated section codes should be filtered out.
Prefer this read-only preview before workspace_subscription_import when the caller needs confirmation or typo recovery.
MCP callers that need per-section selection can subscribe selected preview results by calling workspace_subscription_add with each section.jwId.

tools.name: workspace_subscription_import
tools.returns: { success: Boolean, semester, matchedCodes, unmatchedCodes, addedCount, alreadySubscribedCount, subscription }
notes: Convenience mutation that combines matching with subscription update.
Use catalog_section_match_preview first when the caller needs a dry-run style preview.
Default and the deprecated summary alias return the subscription totals plus feed link information, not the entire subscribed section list.
Request full mode only when the caller explicitly needs the full CalendarSubscription with sections[].

fields: A standalone quick-add dialog searches section codes, course codes, localized course names, and localized teacher names within one semester, shows matching sections as checkbox options, and subscribes the selected sections without entering the batch-import confirmation flow
A separate batch-import dialog accepts pasted course and section codes for one selected semester
Batch subscribe, unsubscribe, and replace/update actions

Subscribed sections are grouped by semester into separate responsive tables ordered from the latest semester to the earliest; the layout stays single-column until an extra-wide viewport, where supporting browsers use native CSS Grid Lanes to pack tables into two columns and other browsers fall back to a row-ordered two-column grid
Long course names and teacher lists are visually truncated inside table rows without changing the complete text shown in the section details dialog
Each table row opens a details dialog with section metadata, a course-homepage link, and an unsubscribe action guarded by a destructive confirmation dialog
Matched quick-add options show course, code, semester, campus, and teachers; already-subscribed sections cannot be selected again
